what: §I s_N/early-warning framework applied to real CryoSat-2 + ITS_LIVE lake data
scope: 3 open-velocity MacAyeal lakes (Mac1/2/3); full 131-lake/ATL15 population gated

sources.cs2_elevation: mrsiegfried/Siegfried2021-GRL data/cs2/proc_out/<lake>/<lake>_elevs.dat (CryoSat-2 monthly in-lake mean elevation, 2010.6-2020.5)
sources.velocity: ITS_LIVE datacubes (anon S3), box-mean surface speed at lake centroid
sources.drainage_dates_note: The vetted full 131-lake volume-change catalogue remains USAP-DC-gated; these 5 marquee lakes have OPEN CS2 series.

Mercer.note: open drainage, no ITS_LIVE velocity coverage

Conway.note: open drainage, no ITS_LIVE velocity coverage

interpretation: Master curve (§I.1): small mixed-sign responses bound |s_N|·(ΔN/N) at the ~1-2%% level -> these trunk lakes sit far from the N_c flotation pole. EWS (§I.3/§I.6): no lake shows the joint rising-variance + high-AC1 CSD signature -> the early-warning returns a true negative on stable ice (no false ungrounding alarm). Both §I probes agree: far from ungrounding.
honest_limits: n=8 annual velocity points per lake is statistically weak for a CSD trend; post-event sample counts are small (1-7). A strong test needs the dense quarterly series and a lake actually approaching flotation. The full 131-lake volume-change catalogue (and an ATL15 re-download, ~7.6 GB) remains USAP-DC/ATL15-gated and would mostly reproduce the existing 1/19 detection.
verdict: REAL DATA (no new download): across 3 resolved drainage events at 3 open-velocity MacAyeal lakes, |Δv/v|≤2.0%% (mixed sign, near the ~1%% noise floor) — no clean universal post-drainage surge; and 0 lakes show the §I joint CSD signature. Via the §I master curve the bounded response places these trunk lakes far from the N_c fold, and the §I early-warning correctly does NOT false-alarm on them. A genuinely-new cross-framework check on real CryoSat-2/ITS_LIVE data; the full ATL15 population extension stays gated.
references: this repo §I (sn_master_curve, spatial_ews), §H.2 (lake_lag_atl15_itslive); ITS_LIVE (Gardner 2022); CryoSat-2; Siegfried & Fricker 2018
